An existing model judges the pretrained checkpoint and every finetune variant on multi-domain completions and task instructions, plus a real-world accuracy probe now treated as a direct signal rather than a demoted one. A future redesign would add in-context binding as the primary test.

Stage five. run_all evaluates the pretrain checkpoint and every finetune variant, writing report_pretrain and report_sft (or report_sft_<variant> per variant). Each judges completions for grammar and coherence, task instructions (explain, how-to, compare, define, answer, advise, summarize, rewrite, list, reason) for coherence and whether the answer follows and is correct, and a factual accuracy probe for correctness, all over seeds spanning the same subject domains generation uses.

Evaluation uses an existing model as judge. It was first reworked from judging only the SFT model on out-of-distribution real-world questions (which produced systematically misleading gibberish-looking scores) to judging the base pretrained model on in-distribution completions and in-world instructions; the SFT checkpoint is then also evaluated separately, so the effect of finetuning is directly visible (see node 30, node 44). It was reworked again once generation dropped the referent-free restriction and broadened to real, multi-domain content (node 41): the fixed seeds, instructions, and probe questions were still bank and forest fragments from the earlier referent-free corpus, so they no longer matched what the model was trained on. The referent_free scoring axis and the knowledge-avoidance probe were also inverted relative to the current goal, since generation now wants the model to be knowledgeable; the axis was dropped from completions and instructions, and the probe was turned into a genuine accuracy check (score correctness, not avoidance), promoted from demoted-and-unreliable to the most direct available signal of what finetuning adds over the base pretrained model, though a model this small is still expected to know very little of it. The processor-not-oracle reframing (node 32) argues this probe should eventually be joined, and possibly outranked, by in-context binding tasks (node 33), which are not yet implemented.
